name: Tafamidis reduced ATTR-CM mortality and cardiovascular hospitalizations
summary: ATTR-ACT anchors durable disease-outcome relevance for preventing further transthyretin amyloidogenesis in an age-associated amyloid disease.
statement: In transthyretin amyloid cardiomyopathy, tafamidis reduced all-cause mortality and cardiovascular-related hospitalizations over 30 months and slowed decline in function and quality of life.
quantitative_note: All-cause mortality was 29.5% with tafamidis versus 42.9% with placebo, HR 0.70 (95% CI 0.51 to 0.96); cardiovascular hospitalization relative risk ratio was 0.68 (95% CI 0.56 to 0.81).
caveats: Tafamidis stabilizes TTR tetramers to prevent amyloidogenesis rather than clearing existing aggregates.
The result is an ATTR-CM disease outcome, not direct evidence of broad aging or systemic aggregate clearance.
The benefit is most interpretable for age-associated transthyretin amyloid disease.
